summary: My journey began in Cambodia when I was 18 years old. My family and I traveled on foot from Cambodia to Thailand, facing many hardships including crossing rivers and encounters with Vietnamese soldiers. Cambodia is where my story started before we sought refuge elsewhere.

After leaving Cambodia, my family and I reached Thailand. It was a difficult part of our journey as refugees traveling on foot. Thailand was our first stop outside Cambodia before we continued on to other countries like Indonesia and Singapore.

Following our time in Thailand, we moved to Indonesia as part of our journey to safety. While in Indonesia and later Singapore, we focused on learning English to prepare for a new life. These countries were important stepping stones before coming to the United States.

Singapore was one of the places my family and I stayed after leaving Thailand and Indonesia. During our time there, we worked hard to learn English, which was crucial for our future in America. Singapore was part of our transition before being sponsored to come to the United States.

The Catholic Church played a vital role in our journey by sponsoring my family and me to come to America. Without their support, our move to the United States might not have been possible. Their sponsorship was a turning point in our lives.

The United States became our new home after a long and difficult journey through Southeast Asia. We first arrived in Alaska, then moved to Minnesota, and finally settled in California. America offered us opportunities to build a new life, learn English, and grow as a family.

Alaska was the first place we arrived in the United States. It was a cold and unfamiliar environment, but it marked the beginning of our American experience. After some time there, we moved on to Minnesota to continue building our new life.

We lived in Minnesota for about a year after arriving in the U.S. It was a meaningful time for our family; we even took a family photo to celebrate a family member winning an award in junior high. Although it was a good experience, we eventually moved to California for more opportunities.

California became our permanent home after Minnesota. We lived in an apartment in San Jose, where I truly began my life as an American. It was challenging at first, especially with the language barrier and discrimination, but I persevered and graduated from high school there.

San Jose was where my family settled when we moved to California. Living there, I attended Yerbabuena High School and faced challenges due to my limited English. Despite the difficulties, San Jose is where I started building my adult life and future.

I attended Yerbabuena High School in San Jose, California. It was tough because I was discriminated against for not knowing English well. However, I worked hard to improve my language skills and eventually graduated, which was a proud moment for me.

My first job in the United States was flipping burgers at Wendy's. It was an important step in starting my adult life and gaining work experience. Later, I was able to open and run my own donut shop and deli for 16 years, building on that early experience.
